preview_text:
The model favours Belgium to win at 64%, with New Zealand granted an 11% chance of victory. The underdog faces a steep climb on a neutral pitch, though the model's assessment leaves meaningful room for an upset or a draw outcome.

The win-loss spread is decidedly wide, reflecting a substantial gap in the statistical profiles. A draw rates as the second-most-likely result at 24%, suggesting the match will not necessarily unfold as a one-sided affair. Belgium's two-to-one advantage over New Zealand signals competitive imbalance, yet draws occur frequently enough in such matchups to warrant note.

The market consensus shifts the favourite's advantage more sharply, pushing Belgium to 75% and compressing both the draw to 16% and New Zealand's chances to 10%. The model and sportsbooks align on the direction but diverge meaningfully on the magnitude: the books view Belgium's superiority as more pronounced than the model does, particularly in assigning lower draw probability. Polymarket prices show similar tilt toward Belgium at 77%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which forecast is more accurate.
